name: Platform-Neutral Design Framework
lever_classification: methodology — which data collection approach to use
consequences: Developing a design framework that prioritizes platform-neutrality will require significant upfront investment in research and development, potentially delaying the project timeline. However, this approach will also enable the creation of more resilient and adaptable digital identity systems.
options: Embedding open standards like FIDO2/WebAuthn and OpenID Connect directly into the AltID architecture
Implementing a modular, plugin-based design that allows for easy integration of platform-agnostic authentication modules
Developing a hybrid approach that combines elements of both, leveraging existing platform-specific APIs while also incorporating platform-neutral components
review: The project's reliance on a single physical location in Copenhagen may inadvertently create a bottleneck in the development process, limiting the team's ability to scale or adapt to changing circumstances.

name: Procurement Strategy
lever_classification: governance — who oversees the review process
consequences: Influencing procurement processes to prioritize platform-neutrality will require building strong relationships with key stakeholders and navigating potential resistance from incumbent suppliers. Successful execution may lead to more resilient digital identity systems, but also increases the risk of supplier pushback.
options: Developing a comprehensive procurement plan that explicitly prioritizes platform-neutral solutions
Engaging with relevant EU bodies and technical working groups to align Danish advocacy with broader European requirements on interoperability and reduced platform lock-in
Focusing on influencing AltID-related design, policy, consultation, or procurement documents to include platform-neutrality language
review: The project's emphasis on procurement intervention may overlook the potential for alternative suppliers to emerge and challenge the existing duopoly.

name: Regulatory Engagement
lever_classification: governance — who oversees the review process
consequences: Engaging with relevant EU bodies and technical working groups will require significant time and resources, potentially diverting attention away from other project objectives. However, successful engagement may lead to more widespread adoption of platform-neutral digital identity systems.
options: Building strong relationships with key stakeholders in the European Digital Identity Wallet framework
Developing a comprehensive understanding of relevant EU regulations and standards, such as eIDAS and NSIS
Focusing on influencing Danish procurement processes and AltID-related design documents
review: The project's reliance on external regulatory frameworks may create uncertainty around the long-term viability of platform-neutral digital identity systems.

name: Technical Demonstrators
lever_classification: methodology — which data collection approach to use
consequences: Developing non-production technical demonstrators will require significant investment in research and development, potentially delaying the project timeline. However, this approach will also enable the creation of more resilient and adaptable digital identity systems.
options: Creating a mobile-oriented demonstrator that authenticates against a simulated Danish public-sector identity-provider environment using open standards
Developing a browser-based or hardware-token-based fallback authentication path that reduces dependence on the mobile OS duopoly
Focusing on creating a hybrid approach that combines elements of both, leveraging existing platform-specific APIs while also incorporating platform-neutral components
review: The project's emphasis on technical demonstrators may overlook the potential for alternative solutions to emerge and challenge the existing digital identity infrastructure.

name: Coalition Building
lever_classification: governance — who oversees the review process
consequences: Building a coalition of stakeholders will require significant time and resources, potentially diverting attention away from other project objectives. However, successful engagement may lead to more widespread adoption of platform-neutral digital identity systems.
options: Engaging with civil-society organisations, privacy advocates, academic researchers, security experts, contingency-planning stakeholders, and sympathetic members of Folketinget's digital policy committee
Developing a comprehensive understanding of relevant Danish and EU digital identity frameworks, including eIDAS and NSIS
Focusing on influencing AltID-related design documents and procurement processes
review: The project's reliance on external stakeholders may create uncertainty around the long-term viability of platform-neutral digital identity systems.

name: Physical Location Strategy
lever_classification: operations — how to sequence the rollout phases
consequences: Establishing a physical base in Copenhagen will require significant investment in infrastructure and personnel, potentially diverting attention away from other project objectives. However, this approach will also enable the creation of more resilient and adaptable digital identity systems.
options: Partnering with a Danish university or independent research institution to access controlled workspace
Establishing a physical presence in Copenhagen through office space or coworking arrangements
Focusing on remote work and virtual collaboration to minimize the need for physical infrastructure
review: The project's reliance on a single physical location may inadvertently create a bottleneck in the development process, limiting the team's ability to scale or adapt to changing circumstances.

name: Financial Planning
lever_classification: governance — who oversees the review process
consequences: Securing adequate funding will require significant time and resources, potentially diverting attention away from other project objectives. However, successful financial planning may lead to more resilient digital identity systems.
options: Developing a comprehensive financing plan covering the remainder of the project
Engaging with relevant EU bodies and technical working groups to align Danish advocacy with broader European requirements on interoperability and reduced platform lock-in
Focusing on influencing AltID-related design documents and procurement processes
review: The project's reliance on external funding may create uncertainty around the long-term viability of platform-neutral digital identity systems.

name: Digital Identity Framework Revisions
lever_classification: methodology — which data collection approach to use
consequences: Revising the Danish eIDAS and NSIS frameworks to accommodate platform-neutral access requirements could directly improve digital sovereignty, but might also trigger a bureaucratic review process that delays adoption.
options: Embed platform-neutrality in the eIDAS2 wallet rollout's technical standards
Develop an annex to the eIDAS framework outlining fallback authentication procedures
Create a separate Danish NSIS regulation emphasizing platform-neutral access requirements
review: Revising the eIDAS and NSIS frameworks to accommodate platform-neutral access requirements might overlook the need for a clear, phased transition plan from the current MitID setup.

name: Public Awareness Campaigns
lever_classification: dissemination — how to communicate project goals and outcomes
consequences: Running public awareness campaigns about digital sovereignty and platform neutrality could raise awareness among Danish citizens, but might also be met with resistance from those who perceive it as an attack on the current system.
options: Launch a targeted social media campaign highlighting the benefits of platform-neutral access
Organize public events and workshops to educate citizens about digital sovereignty risks
Partner with influential Danish figures to promote platform-neutrality through their social networks
review: Public awareness campaigns might overlook the need for clear, technical explanations of platform-neutral access requirements.

name: Institutional Capacity Building
lever_classification: governance — who oversees the review process
consequences: Building institutional capacity within Danish digital identity agencies could improve their ability to handle platform-neutral access requirements, but might also lead to bureaucratic delays.
options: Develop training programs for agency staff on platform-neutral access procedures
Establish a dedicated task force within the Digitaliseringsstyrelsen to oversee platform-neutrality implementation
Create a certification program for agencies that demonstrate proficiency in platform-neutral access requirements
review: Institutional capacity building might overlook the need for clear, measurable goals and performance indicators.

name: International Partnerships
lever_classification: governance — who oversees the review process
consequences: Establishing international partnerships with European bodies could improve Denmark's digital identity infrastructure by leveraging best practices and expertise, but might also lead to cultural and linguistic barriers.
options: Partner with the European Digital Identity Wallet framework to align Danish advocacy with broader EU requirements
Collaborate with other Nordic countries to develop a shared platform-neutral access standard
Join international working groups focused on digital identity and platform-neutrality
review: International partnerships might overlook the need for clear, measurable goals and performance indicators.

name: Standards Development
lever_classification: methodology — which data collection approach to use
consequences: Developing standards for platform-neutral access could improve digital sovereignty in Denmark, but might also lead to delays due to the need for consensus among stakeholders.
options: Create a new Danish standard outlining platform-neutrality requirements
Update existing eIDAS and NSIS standards to include platform-neutral access procedures
Develop a certification program for products that meet platform-neutrality standards
review: Standards development might overlook the need for clear, measurable goals and performance indicators.

name: Public Procurement Reform
lever_classification: governance — who oversees the review process
consequences: Reforming public procurement processes in Denmark could improve digital sovereignty by reducing dependence on foreign technology suppliers, but might also lead to resistance from existing operators.
options: Develop a new public procurement standard emphasizing platform-neutrality requirements
Update existing procurement procedures to include platform-neutral access criteria
Create a certification program for vendors that meet platform-neutrality standards
review: Public procurement reform might overlook the need for clear, measurable goals and performance indicators.

name: Digital Identity Governance Reform
lever_classification: governance — who oversees the review process
consequences: Reforming digital identity governance in Denmark could improve digital sovereignty by reducing dependence on foreign technology suppliers, but might also lead to resistance from existing operators.
options: Develop a new governance framework emphasizing platform-neutrality requirements
Update existing governance procedures to include platform-neutral access criteria
Create a certification program for agencies that meet platform-neutrality standards
review: Digital identity governance reform might overlook the need for clear, measurable goals and performance indicators.

name: Fallback Access Architecture
lever_classification: methodology — which data collection approach to use
consequences: Implementing an open standards-based fallback authentication path reduces dependence on Apple/Google platform integrity APIs and proprietary push-notification infrastructure.
options: Use FIDO2/WebAuthn for secure authentication
Implement OpenID Connect on GrapheneOS or a comparable Android-compatible platform
Explore browser-based or hardware-token-based fallback access
review: The project's focus on open standards and fallback access may overlook the need to address existing certification requirements and legacy system integrations, potentially creating new complexity for future upgrades.

name: Procurement Language Development
lever_classification: procurement — which data collection approach to use
consequences: Incorporating platform-neutrality and fallback access language into procurement documents reduces dependence on foreign technology suppliers.
options: Develop specific requirements for platform-neutral digital identity systems
Include language on certified fallback authentication paths in procurement contracts
Create a framework for supplier-neutral design principles
review: The project's emphasis on procurement language development may not fully address the need to engage with existing operators and stakeholders who may resist changes to their business models.

name: Regulatory Engagement Strategy
lever_classification: regulatory — which data collection approach to use
consequences: Engaging with EU bodies and technical working groups influences broader European requirements on interoperability, portability, resilience, and reduced platform lock-in.
options: Build relationships with key EU stakeholders
Participate in relevant EU technical working groups
Develop a strategy for influencing EU digital identity policy
review: The project's focus on regulatory engagement may overlook the need to address existing Danish regulations and laws, potentially creating new complexities for future implementation.

name: Coalition Consolidation
lever_classification: advocacy — which data collection approach to use
consequences: Consolidating a coalition of stakeholders supports the project's advocacy efforts and influences procurement processes.
options: Build relationships with civil society organizations
Establish partnerships with academic researchers and security experts
Develop a strategy for influencing digital policy in Denmark
review: The project's focus on coalition consolidation may overlook the need to address existing power dynamics and conflicts of interest within the coalition, potentially creating new challenges for future collaboration.
